headline: Samsung Weighs Multi-Year Deals to Ease Memory Chip Crunch Fears
summary: SK Hynix Inc., Samsung and Micron Technology Inc. together dominate the supply of memory chips globally.  The three have shifted production in recent years toward a specialized form of memory intended for use in Nvidia Corp.’s in-demand AI accelerators, leading to a shortfall in output of more conventional storage.

headline: Micron’s New HBM4 And PCIe Gen6 SSDs Test Lofty AI Valuation
summary: Micron Technology has started volume production of its latest HBM4 memory aimed at Nvidia's next generation AI platforms. The company is also rolling out what it describes as the industry's first mass produced PCIe Gen6 data center SSDs for AI workloads. These launches follow the completion of Micron's acquisition and expansion of a major Taiwan facility focused on advanced DRAM and HBM for AI applications. For investors watching NasdaqGS:MU, the news comes during a period of strong recent...

headline: Micron Earnings Face High Expectations as Its Stock Keeps Rising
summary: Micron shares have soared more than 60% to start the year, the sixth-best performance in the S&P 500 Index, after jumping 239% in 2025.  The biggest US maker of memory chips is benefiting from heavy spending on artificial intelligence computing that’s driving demand for memory and storage components, as are high-flying peers Sandisk Corp. and Western Digital Corp.

headline: Micron Q2 earnings, Q3 outlook surpass Wall Street's estimates
summary: Micron (MU) reported second quarter results on Wednesday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $12.20 (compared to analyst estimates of $9.00), and adjusted revenue came in at $23.86 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $19.74 billion). Micron's third quarter earnings and revenue forecasts blow Wall Street's estimates out of the water. Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a closer look at the numbers.

content.title: Micron Q2 earnings, Q3 outlook surpass Wall Street's estimates
content.description:
Micron (MU) reported second quarter results on Wednesday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $12.20 (compared to analyst estimates of $9.00), and adjusted revenue came in at $23.86 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $19.74 billion).

Micron's third quarter earnings and revenue forecasts blow Wall Street's estimates out of the water.

Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a closer look at the numbers.

Micron's third quarter earnings and revenue forecasts blow Wall Street's estimates out of the water. Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a closer look at the numbers.

headline: Micron (NASDAQ:MU) Delivers Strong Q1 Numbers
summary: Memory chips maker Micron (NYSE:MU) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 196% year on year to $23.86 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 40.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Micron (NASDAQ:MU) Delivers Strong Q1 Numbers
content.summary: Memory chips maker Micron (NYSE:MU) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 196% year on year to $23.86 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 40.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Memory chipmaker Micron's revenue beats estimates on demand surge from AI boom
summary: Chipmaker Micron Technology forecast third-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations on Wednesday, betting ‌on a surge in demand for its memory ‌chips used in artificial intelligence hardware. The resulting growth in AI data center capacity is fueling a sharp rise in demand for advanced ‌memory and storage.

headline: Micron Earnings Were Extraordinary. The Stock Is Dropping.
summary: Micron Technology  reported exceptional second-quarter earnings results Wednesday afternoon.  Adjusted earnings-per-share soared to $12.20, well beyond Wall Street’s consensus estimate of $9.19, and up from $1.56 last year.  Guidance for the third quarter is equally stunning, with a projection of $33.5 billion in sales and a whopping 81% gross margin, even better than  Nvidia’s  Adjusted EPS are expected to rise to $19.15, a 903% jump from the year before.

content.title: Micron Earnings Were Extraordinary. The Stock Is Dropping.
content.summary: Micron Technology  reported exceptional second-quarter earnings results Wednesday afternoon.  Adjusted earnings-per-share soared to $12.20, well beyond Wall Street’s consensus estimate of $9.19, and up from $1.56 last year.  Guidance for the third quarter is equally stunning, with a projection of $33.5 billion in sales and a whopping 81% gross margin, even better than  Nvidia’s  Adjusted EPS are expected to rise to $19.15, a 903% jump from the year before.

headline: Micron forecasts strong revenue on AI boom, shares fall on higher spending plan
summary: Micron Technology forecast third-quarter revenue well above Wall Street expectations after posting a sharp jump in the ‌second quarter on booming demand for memory chips used in ‌artificial intelligence systems, while tighter supply drove record earnings.  Micron aims to spend more than $25 billion this fiscal year and said this could ‌rise further in 2027 ⁠as expansion in manufacturing facilities could result in construction-related expense climbing by more than $10 billion from a year ⁠ago.  Customers are committing to long-term data center investments as technology companies race toward artificial general intelligence.

Micron aims to spend more than $25 billion this fiscal year and said this could ‌rise further in 2027 ⁠as expansion in manufacturing facilities could result in construction-related expense climbing by more than $10 billion from a year ⁠ago. headline: Micron forecasts strong revenue on AI boom; higher spending plan knocks shares
summary: Micron Technology forecast third-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations after posting a sharp jump in the second quarter on booming demand ‌for memory chips used in artificial intelligence systems, while tighter supply drove record ‌earnings.  But its shares fell 5% in extended trading on Wednesday after Micron said it was boosting its 2026 capital ​spending plan by $5 billion to keep up with the rising demand.  "Construction activity ‌is really driving a very ⁠significant increase in our overall capex," Sumit Sadana, Micron's chief business officer told Reuters in an interview.

But its shares fell 5% in extended trading on Wednesday after Micron said it was boosting its 2026 capital ​spending plan by $5 billion to keep up with the rising demand.  "Construction activity ‌is really driving a very ⁠significant increase in our overall capex," Sumit Sadana, Micron's chief business officer told Reuters in an interview.

headline: Micron Q2 revenue nearly triples; shares fall 5% in premarket on spending plan
summary: Investing.com -- Micron Technology’s fiscal second-quarter revenue nearly tripled year-on-year and earnings per share rose nearly eightfold, but shares fell after the chipmaker said it would spend over $25 billion on new manufacturing facilities in fiscal 2026, about $5 billion more than prior forecasts.
